entry.title: Re: Not option of Country- India in "Mass Upload Utility"...
entry.content: Looks like this app is developed only for the 4 countries that we see in the drop down and SAP recommends using other options to upload the commodity code. Basically, no option to change anything in the app, or nothing is missing from configuration / activation perspective in the Fiori app.

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : Table Control Creation
entry.content:
Hello,

Table controls are usually classic SAP GUI ALV or Table Control, that you maintain through PERSONAS. Example VL03N 

 

If you have to create one, you must go on SAP GUI, create a Z tcode, a custom program behind with the handling of an ALV.

Then in your SMEN FLAVOR, you create button that calls your custom transaction with the ALV and here you have your table control that you can custom using PERSONAS.

Even better : if you have OPENUI5 librairies available, you can build a APPLET to include a fiori like component to display whatever you want to display.

Good luck to you!

entry.title: Re: how to trigger handler class read method in RAP OData...
entry.content:
Hi Junwu

In my current case, CPI is not sending the material data. CDS view is blank, so I would like to know if

I can pass the payload in S/4 MDG itself and test?

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to read approvers via script on Sap CPQ?
entry.content:
Hi, 

we created a custom action in CPQ 2.0 as post action after approval. 

We added the following code: 

from Scripting.Quote import MessageLevel
from datetime import datetime

history = context.Quote.GetApprovalHistory()

approvers = []  # Approver name from ApprovalHistory
enrichedApprovers = []  # enriched Userdata ready for consumption
# === NEU: Finde den letzten "Submitted for Approval"-Zeitpunkt ===
submitted_timestamps = [
    entry.SubmissionDate for entry in history if entry.Status == "Submitted for Approval"
]
latest_submitted = max(submitted_timestamps) if submitted_timestamps else None

for entry in history:
    if entry.Status == 'Approved' and latest_submitted and entry.ResolvingDate > latest_submitted:
        approvers.append(entry.Approver)

unique_approvers = list(set(approvers))

I hope this helps!

BR, 
Tobias

entry.title: Re: Delivery address in Purchase order
entry.content:
Why Case 1 and Case 2 behave differently because

In Case 1, when you edit the delivery address immediately after creating the PO, the system has not yet “refreshed” the item buffer. Each item is still treated as having its own address reference, so the system doesn’t prompt you to update all items.

In Case 2, by switching tabs and coming back, you force ME21N to refresh the item buffer. At that point, SAP recognizes that multiple items share the same address number. When you change the address, the system correctly asks whether you want to update all items.


Workaround: After creating the PO, switch tabs (or save and re‑open) before editing the delivery address if you want the popup to appear.

Permanent Fix: This is standard behavior in ME21N; if it’s disruptive, you can raise an SAP incident. SAP may provide a note or correction if they consider it a bug in your release.

Alternative: If you want consistent address updates across items, consider using the header delivery address instead of item‑level addresses, so changes automatically apply to all items.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance operation status is changed back to "In P...
entry.content:
Hello,

you are probably missing the Calculation Key in the operation. Please add a value in here and test it again.

I assume that the missing calculation key comes from the corresponding task list in the maintenance plan. What you can do: Create a profile for task list and add a calculation key in there. When creating a new task list, assign this profile to the task list and some fields like the calculation key are filled by default for each operation.

Se alguem puder me ajudar fico grato.

entry.title: Re: Business Configuration Transport from DEV to Test / Prod in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content: Hi Ciaran, is there any improvement on this regards? What is the current status if we talk about the "transport mechanism" in Subscription Billing? Thanks a lot and best regards, Doris

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Directly from SAP System – Using Staging Tables
entry.content:
Hi all,  

Over the past few months, our customers have asked us whether there is any way to get more transparency on the selected data for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System. Customers have also asked whether there are any opportunities to adjust the selected data based on their requirements. 

The selection process for migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System is very complex. This made it difficult to adjust or even view the selected data in the Migrate Your Data app. 

However, with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition CE2602 and upcoming Private Cloud Edition release, we are happy to announce our new feature Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage for Direct Transfer.  

Most of you are familiar with the selection process of migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, an RFC connection is used to connect to the source system. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a communication arrangement for communication scenario SAP_COM_0816 is used.  

Figure 1: Selection Process without Staging Tables  

Why use staging tables as intermediate storage? 

With this new feature, the Migrate Your Data app can create staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System in a remote SAP HANA schema and can store the selected data there. You can then adjust the values directly in the relevant database tables before continuing with the migration process. This option provides increased transparency for the selected data, as well as providing the opportunity to adjust data directly in the relevant database tables if required. 

Figure 2: Selection Process with Staging Tables  

You activate this feature when creating a migration project. During step 3, Staging Tables (Optional), you activate the feature and specify the relevant connection to the remote SAP HANA schema in the Database Connection field. 

You then need to activate staging tables for the relevant migration objects. 

Note that using staging tables is completely optional! 

When using staging tables as intermediate storage, the selection process is divided into two steps. In the first step, the relevant data is extracted from the ECC system to the staging tables. This step is triggered in the Migrate Your Data app using the action Extract to Staging Tables. 

In the second step the data is transferred from the staging table to the Migrate Your Data app. Again, this step is triggered in the Migrate Your Data app, this time using the action Transfer from Staging Tables. 

Once you have extracted data to the staging tables, you can not only view the data as a whole in the remote SAP HANA schema, but you can also adjust the data according to your requirements. That gives you more flexibility in preparing the data for the migration.   

Figure 3: Contents of staging table  

Note that the staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System cannot be used with the staging tables for the approach Migrate Data Using Staging Tables. The staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Using Staging tables are based on the template files for a migration object provided by SAP. The staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System are based on the data model of the migration objects.  

Let us know how you might use the staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System and feel free to comment below!   

All the best for your project!  

Philipp

entry.title: Implementing Authorization Checks for Data Modification Using CDS Access Controls
entry.content:
Introduction

Using CDS Access control we can restrict data selection access. It allows you to put checks on the data using standard authorization object and limiting values for the same

e.g. This is one of the standard access control for Profit Center

In this blog, we will look at how access control can be used to control UI‑level modifications—for example, managing the visibility of Create, Edit, and Delete options—without the need for additional custom coding.

Backend Implementation

To begin, create a CDS view that lists all business objects requiring this authorization control. Add an entry for each operation—such as create or edit. For demo, I’ve included activity '02' (change).

Next, define an Access Control object that links your CDS view to the relevant authorization object. This authorization object will be maintained for the associated Fiori application. Set the required activity type based on the configuration in your CDS access control.
In this example, I’ve used the standard CDS view I_PROFITCENTER, but you can replace it with your own entity that needs authorization‑based restrictions.

 

When a user with the correct authorization views the CDS data,

User not having authorization for activity will see content as below

 

Once the CDS view is ready, include it as an association within your main entity that is exposed via your OData service:

...

association[0..1] to ZTEST_BO_AUTH as _BOAuth

on cdsviewname = 'I_PROFITCENTER'

{

...,

_BOAuth

}

Applying Authorization in the Fiori UI

 

In your Fiori application, bind the visibility or enabled property of Create/Edit/Delete buttons to the isEditable property provided by this child association:

{/ZTEST_BO_AUTH('I_ProfitCenter')/isEditable}

Get this entityset loaded at the beginning.

                        var oModel =  this.getView().getModel();

                     oModel.read("/ZTEST_CBO_AUTH", {

                    urlParameters: {

                        "$filter":"BusinessObjectID eq 'I_PROFITCENTER'",

                        "$orderby":"isEditable desc"

                    },

                    success: function (oData) {

                        if (oData?.results ) {

                              var bEditable = oData.results[0].isEditable; // boolean conversion

                        }

When a user without activity '02' opens the app, the Edit button will no longer appear.

Conclusion

Using CDS Access Controls is one of the clean way to enforce consistent authorization across backend services and Fiori UIs. Instead of scattering checks across controllers and handlers, developers can define access rules once and rely on CDS to enforce them everywhere. This low‑code technique reduces maintenance effort and makes the implementation much easier.

entry.title: Excited to Join SAP Community
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Talicia Sanders, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I'm a combat veteran with and IT background Very excited to be learn and explore SAP.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in Supply chain management, and Procurement.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because Technology is revolving door, I enjoy learning new things, I'm so passion about technology and continue growing my career.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in Procurement and Supply chain management.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,  

Talicia Sanders

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 2 - The JMS Revolution

@Patrick_Schaaf I was also looking for it 🙂

br

Mateusz

entry.title: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition
entry.content:
This blog provides a curated collection of how‑to guides, practical tips, and step‑by‑step instructions based on real requests we have received from users working with the Discovery Package for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition. Its purpose is to help others facing similar questions complete their tasks more efficiently. In addition, several entries highlight known limitations or issues encountered in the process—offering transparency and guidance on how to navigate them. The hub continues to grow as new questions and requests come in.

Topics covered

  • Client Copy 

  • Change ADT to logon to a different client

  • Http error 429

 

Client Copy

It is possible to run a local client copy in the Discovery package system using TA SCCLN. TA SCC_CLIENT_SIZE can be used to check on the size of a client. Due to HANA compression, this is only an approximation if more than one client is filled. The tool also supports selective size determination for specified tables. As an example client 100 size info looks like this:  

Some useful tips: 

  • Client copies must no longer be executed from the target client. Instead, we recommend to run the Client Copy tool from a third client which is not affected by the tool. For example, when copying from client 100 to 200, you could run the tool in client 000. 

  • The client copy always locks the target client. By default, the source client is also locked. You may choose to not lock the source client at the risk of concurrent write access to the source client resulting in inconsistent copies. A client lock now not only prevents new logins using SAP GUI, but also for HTTP access. 

Here is an example of creating a new Client 101 and copying Client 100 into it. Using profile SAP_ALL copies almost all information. This is the pic after the copy.  

 

Change ADT to logon to a different client

If you want to logon ADT to a different client than the default client 100 (e.g. after a client copy) you can configure it as follows: 

In the https logon method, ADT uses the client specified in the profile parameter login/system_client -- client 100 in our system. This can be changed to a different client in TA RZ10 and requires a restart (see sevice requests in SAP for Me). Afterwards, ADT/Fiori/WebGUI will use this one as default logon client.  In Fiori and WebGui you can still specify other clients on the logon screen or by adding ?sap-client=xxx to the URL. 

 

Http Error 429

When an HTTP 429 error occurs, it means the HTTP client has sent too many requests within a short period of time. 

The TDD Discovery Package system is configured with a fixed capacity of 32 CPUs and 256 GB RAM. 
If an HTTP 429 error appears, you need to reduce the number of parallel requests, for example by lowering the number of simultaneous logons. 

Please note that the TDD system is not designed for load or performance testing.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud with Spartacus installation error
entry.content:
Most of the time this error comes with wrong java version.

I faced similar issue and I used below command. Then it told me that i am using wrong java version.

install.bat -r cx -Dscan --stacktrace 

After correting the java version i am able to install my workspace.

entry.title: Mark role as “OBSOLETE” in PFCG
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts.

I can’t market role as obsolete in PFCG. This menu item is missing.

Moreover, in another systems this menu item is present.

Product - SAP NETWEAVER AS ABAP  

Release - 7.11

SP \ FP Stack - 14 (01/2015)

Product Version - SAP EHP1 FOR SAP NW ABAP 7.1

Installed components:

SAP_BASIS        711       0020     SAPKB71120    SAP Basis Component

SAP_ABA           711       0020     SAPKA71120    Cross-Application Component

PI_BASIS           711       0020     SAPK-71120INPIBASIS Basis Plug-In

ST-PI     2008_1_710     0037     SAPK-71037INSTPI      SAP Solution Tools Plug-In

ST-A/PI 01W_710          0000     -             Servicetools for other App./Netweaver2007

 

Maybe you know how to fix it? I will appreciate it.

entry.title: Need to Keep Leading Zeros in SAP when using Data Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
Hello

I am using SAP Migration Cockpit to load into SAP Public Cloud. I realized that for field BPEXT (external partner number), leading zeros are removed which is a serious issue as i need it to identify my SAP partner when receiving data from CRM. For instance CRM sends 000000314 and SAP keeps 314, no automatic match is possible.

I tried text & special formatting, to no avail unfortunately, can you please help ? Many thanks

entry.title: Re: PATCH operation failed on API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
entry.content:
Hello, @DEB1989 

The API_SALES_ORDER_SRV is external service doesn't support deep structures, which prevents them from being updated simultaneously with a single PATCH request.
One possible option is to create a batch request where described two requests for two different entities (A_SalesOrder, A_SalesOrderItem). Please refer to the examples provided in the screenshots and code listing.

1. Batch request should has POST HTTP method, URL example #1 URL

2. Set required Headers parameters. Please note the part of value which underlined in red: boundary = {name_batch}
#2 Headers

 3. Code listing of body (json). Provide the same name_batch like in previous step, and declare new boundary like on the second line boundary={name_boundary}

#3 Body

If you have any questions or problems, please let me know.

Best regards,
Aliaksandr.

entry.title: Re: staff scheduling on user level
entry.content:
Dear @Jana_Hauck,

In Public Cloud you generally can’t do HR-style staff rostering plan individual employee shifts + match/validate skills per person. Public Cloud is the digital core for execution (finance/logistics/service) while shift scheduling + skills-based rostering is delivered by dedicated workforce solutions

SAP options that do cover shift rostering + skills:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling is positioned exactly for demand-driven, skills-based, compliant shift scheduling
  • SAP Time and Attendance Management by WorkForce Software explicitly includes scheduling plus time/attendance/absence compliance
  • SAP Workforce Forecasting and Scheduling by WorkForce Software is also a dedicated scheduling product forecast + optimized schedules

Maintain skills for individual user this depends heavily on which kind of skills you mean:

Option A. Project staffing / professional services

Public Cloud does support employee qualifications for project staffing/resource management skills-like attributes used in project resource planning. That’s not shift rostering, it’s project/resource planning

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/configuring-managing-resources_af6b6c75-45ef-4b76-8722-8f7be580394c

Option B. Service technicians dispatching

If your shifts are actually field service assignments, SAP Field Service Management matches service call/activity requirements with technician skills on the planning/dispatch board

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_plan_dispatch/plan-dispatch-overview.html

When Public Cloud needs workforce master data, SAP supports replicating the Workforce Person to Public Edition using SAP Master Data Integration (MDI)

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8d2cbcdb056e4461b313fbcb68160896.html

Summary:

  • Shift-by-shift employee rostering + skills-based scheduling NOT in Public Cloud core. I suggest you consider it use SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling or WorkForce Software products
  • Skills/qualifications inside Public Cloud exist mainly for project staffing/resource management

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Production connector - convert string msgs to JSON
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are getting msgs from Shop floor (PLC) but they are coming into the string format we are not having any way to change the string msgs to JSON/Structure format in production connector functions. 

Does anyone have any idea about how we can convert any string msg to Json format. 

quick help will be appreciated. 

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: Handling Long Text – Migration Perspective
entry.content:
I have used the function /BODS/READ_TEXT to extract long texts from STXL. Now I have 9 rows in my target table showing me each line of the long text from a document info record. My migration object is "Document info record" staging table approach.

The migration object target structure for the long text is defined as data type STRING in SAP which results in a data type LONG in BODS.

How do I get the multiple lines of the function /BODS/READ_TEXT into a single field of data type LONG in BODS?

entry.title: SAP DM - Message Subscription picks only first instance in an structure array
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using structure array in the schema, and we are expecting multiple components in the structure array. But whenever shop floor sends a message with multiple components using subscriptions (triggers) then only first components of array is getting reported in SAP DM, rest others are getting ignored.

Has anyone also faced this issue while using the schema in triggers. or if anyone has any solution to it, please feel free to share

Quick help will be appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: API Purchase Order Item Note
entry.content:
I would like to use the API_PURCHASEORDER_2 odatav4 API to mass upload the item long text on purchase Order.

Following documentation on api.sap.com and help.sap.com I was able to create a note on the Purchase Order Item. However, it appends to existing text, it doesn't overwrite. Is there a way to overwrite?

Thank you

Julien

you missunderstood me. I know that you cannot create Dimensions in AMs. 

What I was trying to say is that we have a table with the name of the AM and also of the user who can access it. There we use the model name and a fictive criterion to allow the access. Here is the table.

entry.title: Re: Contingent Worker/External to Employee Conversions through Onboarding

We have to manually hire the person in via "Add New Employee", although ours is much more manual to avoid the purging urgency; I don't know if our process is any better. I am curious though if anyone has found a better solution for contingent worker to permanent employee conversions.

entry.title: Re: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content: Just other day one of my colleague asked about Inspection process in EH&S. Is this functionality available in latest S/4 HANA Private Cloud editions? Also, the Audit & Inspection Management is part of Worker Safety license ?

entry.title: Re: Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Ryan,

the changes are still traceable in the app Track Changes. Use the app finder to launch the app, then select either by process ID or by business partner to get an overview of changes. To see changed fields and values in detail, select one of the changes.

Example: all business partners changed with process 52.

 

Example: all changes applied to business partner 12300051 with process 52.

Kind regards
Andreas

entry.title: 🇧🇷 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Londrina, Brazil)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam session, you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation to speed up your development with ABAP Cloud.

Join us for this day event of developer networking and hands-on learning at the Teatro Colégio Londrinense - Avenida Juscelino Kubitscheck - Quebec, Londrina - State of Paraná, Brazil.

You can make yourself familiar with the prerequisites and the learning content through the CodeJam content repository on GitHub - TBD.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Wednesday, April 8th 2026, in Londrina, Brazil at the SAP Brasil offices.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman .

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: TBD

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. If you decide not to come, please make sure to unregister using the "No" button in the Will you be attending? box to open up your seat for others.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio skill not showing in our SuccessFactors
entry.content: This is now fixed. We had to manually asign the skill to the environment.

entry.title: Re: SAP Project Systems -BAPIs (A quick overview)

entry.title: Automated email that class added
entry.content:
Hello,

We currently have a Class Request – Demand Met report that is automatically sent to our team inbox. As I review the report, I’m noticing that many learners who submit class requests simply want to be notified when the next class becomes available.

When we add new class sessions, is there a way to automatically notify those requestors that a class is now available and that they can go ahead and register?

entry.title: 30 Years of SAP Partnership and Certified Excellence: cbs a Trusted Partner for SAP Transformation
entry.content:
With more than 30 years of SAP experience and as a multiple-time certified operations partner, cbs - Corporate Business Solutions Unternehmensberatung GmbH - supports industrial companies through complex SAP S/4HANA, cloud, and AI projects. In this interview, cbs explains how industry focus, quality standards, and end-to-end services ensure reliable digital transformation.

 

Can you briefly introduce your company and explain which services you offer?

cbs, Corporate Business Solutions, designs digital end-to-end business process solutions for international industrial clients. With over 3,000 global projects, more than 30 years of market presence, dedicated industry know-how, and a unique, comprehensive offering for business transformation, we support our customers in establishing the next generation of enterprise solutions. Our global consulting organization includes the logistics subsidiary Leogistics and the HR consultancy Projekt0708, our own near- and offshore centers, and a strong partner network. In addition to its headquarters in Heidelberg, cbs is present at more than 40 offices worldwide. More than 1,700 employees across Europe, Asia, and the Americas manage projects and SAP operations for customers worldwide. From SAP strategy to project management and change management to implementation and application management. Business consulting is also a rapidly growing area for us. Here, we deal with topics such as target operating models and inventory optimization.

 

You have been working with SAP for 30 years since its founding. How would you describe your experience so far, and are there any special events or milestones in this partnership that stand out in your memory?

Our founders were former SAP employees. As a result, we had excellent relationships with SAP from day one. This led to us being recommended to customers time and time again, and we lived up to these recommendations by delivering successful projects. It is also always important for us to understand which products or solutions SAP is developing so that we can make informed decisions about where to invest. We focused on GTS early on, trained employees, and carried out successful projects. We passed on the references to SAP so that we could do more business together.

The same applies to SAP S/4 HANA. We also focused on this topic early on, sending employees to Walldorf to participate in product testing, contribute our knowledge, and build new knowledge. We carried out initial projects at an early stage and then led the first SAP S/4HANA migration at Viessmann, a globally active manufacturing company. This was a showcase that DSAG presented at its 2019 annual congress together with the CIO of Viessmann and Christian Klein. We have a good relationship with the SAP Executive Board. We maintain close contact, and Christian Klein and Thomas Saueressig have attended our events on several occasions.  


In 2015, you decided for the first time to have your service portfolio certified by SAP - initially for SAP Business Suite solutions operations and, from 2021 onward, for SAP S/4HANA solutions operations. What motivated you to take this step at the time, and how do you evaluate this decision today?

It is in cbs's DNA to develop and build on its own topics. However, this means that we have little opportunity or knowledge of how our competitors are performing in these areas. On the other hand, we lacked a “seal of quality” to give our customers a certain degree of security. We were able to achieve both with the certification. The audits enabled us to identify areas for improvement, and passing the certification gave us proof we can use, especially when acquiring new customers. We have now completed the certification for the fifth time and still see the added value that certification brings us in the areas mentioned above. 

 

 

From your perspective, what has changed for the better since participating in the SAP Certification Program for Operations Partners?

Whereas knowledge of SAP products/modules used to be the main focus, the program has now shifted to emphasize the IT service process. I think this is absolutely essential and appropriate for certification as an SAP Operations Partner. At the same time, the program is adapted each time to reflect current relevant topics, such as cloud, RISE, Clean Core, or AI.


What makes your company stand out in particular, and how do you support different industries in their digital transformation?

Our industry focus is on the manufacturing sector. This includes traditional manufacturing companies, machine and plant manufacturers, electronics manufacturers, automotive suppliers, as well as customers in the process industry, pharmaceutical industry, and medical technology. We are convinced that it adds value for the customer when we understand the processes, are familiar with the problems typical of the industry, and have already solved them.

In this industry, many customers are still on their way to SAP S/4 HANA. In the context of this topic, however, a complete transformation project is often initiated so that additional SAP solutions are also introduced. Current examples include SAP EWM, Business Data Cloud, and SAP SuccessFactors.

We offer our customers all of this from a single source. To successfully implement large projects, our program and project managers now have comprehensive PMO support. These services handle test and cut-over management, for example. We have established our own change management division because we have seen that change management consultants in the market often lack SAP knowledge and understanding of SAP projects. We also offer consulting for the entire SAP Toolchain.

We are of the “Selective Data Transition Engagement”. Our cbs ET Enterprise Transformer helps to migrate data from ERP Systems (or other sources) to the new S/4 HANA System in a selective approach. Our application management services are also used in S/4 projects. Since projects often run for several years, but customers want to use their own employees in the project, we take over the management of the existing SAP landscape or provide support in the new landscape once the customer is live.

Apart from SAP S/4 projects, we have a large division in the field of BTP and AI. Here, we digitize processes that were not previously mapped in SAP at all, but rather across multiple systems, and in some cases also involved intermediate steps on paper. We bring these together in a SAP BTP solution, thereby accelerating the process, improving quality, and increasing transparency. We are increasingly using AI to further simplify and accelerate processes. With our AI-based text recognition, we achieve recognition rates of more than 98% and can create the corresponding SAP documents, e.g., sales order, purchase order, or invoice, directly from a piece of paper.

 
SAP is consistently aligning its strategy toward the cloud and encouraging partners to actively support their customers in migration. How are you currently supporting your customers in their cloud transformation, and do you plan to place an even stronger focus on cloud services in the future?

Functionally, we cover all SAP solutions that our customers need, whether on-premise or in the cloud. We are one of the few providers that can offer customers all services if they want to implement a full cloud solution (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP CX, SmartRecruiters, SAP SuccessFactors, Concur, Ariba, IBP, DM, SAP BTP, etc.). Our strategy is to deliver cloud projects whenever possible. For example, if we are short on resources and have to decide between a cloud project for one customer and an on-prem project for another, we would go with the cloud project. As the functionality is rising with every release, we recommend that every customer check if a cloud solution fits the requirements. From our point of view, the cloud solution is the go-to solution for every customer.

 

What significance do the certifications have for your marketing activities and for communication with your customers?

The certification provides us with external proof of our performance, our knowledge, and our service processes. We are always happy to use this in our acquisition processes.

 

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: Re: Packaging Instruction Determination Not Triggering Di...
entry.content:
I got it. It is necessary to activate “Start automatically” in Maintain Packing Transaction Profile, in addition to “Pack instruction selection dialog”.

With this configuration, when creating the inbound delivery and accessing the Pack button, then selecting the Packaging Proposal, the dialog is displayed as expected.

entry.title: Re: Vibe Coding my 1st SAP Digital Manufacturing POD Plugin with AI
entry.content: @ThanosG correct.

entry.title: Fiori Migration to SAP BTP: Best Practice for Multi-Backend (Region-Based) Routing Without SAP Gatew
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am working on a Fiori migration project and would like guidance on the recommended architecture for handling multiple backend systems by region after moving to SAP BTP.

Current  Architecture

  • An on-premise SAP Gateway hosts a set of Fiori apps.

  • The Gateway forwards OData calls to multiple backend systems (ECC) based on system alias.

  • This setup allows region-based routing, but currently all backends are technically reachable, and multiple backends may be called during app execution.

Target Architecture

  • Decommission the on-premise SAP Gateway system.

  • Host Fiori apps on SAP BTP and expose them via SAP Build Work Zone.

  • Keep the same backend ECC systems (one per region).

  • No central Gateway system remains for system-alias-based routing.

Our Proposed Approach

  • Define one SAP BTP Destination per backend system (e.g. one per region).

  • Use the same destination names across subaccounts (DEV / QAS / PRD), with destination details varying per subaccount.

  • Configure the Fiori app to call all destinations and aggregate the results on the UI side.

     

Questions

  1. Is this approach (calling all backends and aggregating results in the UI) considered a good practice on SAP BTP?

  2. Are there recommended alternatives for region-based backend routing without a central SAP Gateway?

  3. How is user-region–based backend selection typically handled in BTP-centric Fiori architectures?

Any reference architectures or SAP-recommended patterns would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Managing Performance Forms for Employees on Global As...
entry.content:
Hi @DP4 

With the available business rule scenario Trigger onPostSave Events for Job Information, you can launch a Performance Management form. One possible option would be using as a condition Event Reason = Add Global Assignment so when that happens a new form is created. 

The steps on how to configure it are documented here: 

2530838 - Initiate PM Form On Job Change Event: How to Launch a New Performance Form Based on a Job Info Change https://me.sap.com/notes/2530838/E

As for the existing performance goals,  the Manage Individual Talent Data Transfer tool, where you can transfer data from an inactive employment (source) into a target employment would not be applicable since the Home assignment is paused (dormant) which does not qualify as inactive employment. 

However, the option Transfer Goal Between Users would allow to transfer the performance goals between the userID (home) and userID-1 (host).

Hope this will help, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Re: Using an alternative UOM in "My Purchase Requisition"...
entry.content: Thanks for your insights @HenrikeGrtecke and @Chris1973

entry.title: Withholding Tax is not being calculated when posting invoices
entry.content:
During the posting of incoming invoices. When Section Code is maintained in the Basic Data tab, the system fails to calculate withholding tax. However, when the Section Code is removed, the withholding tax is calculated correctly.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax is not being calculated when posting...
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Please find the details below:

  • If the section code is not passed during invoice posting, the system performs TDS calculation directly on the invoice amount, which is the standard and expected system behavior.

  • If the section code is passed at the time of invoice posting, the system evaluates the supplier-level exemption threshold and triggers TDS calculation only after the cumulative transaction amount exceeds the configured exemption limit.

Kindly refer the KBA 3703336 - Inconsistent 194Q Withholding Tax calculation when posting supplier invoices via Create Incoming Invoices app for more information.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Joule - Performance and Goals Agent
entry.content:
We turned on the Joule performance and goals agent, but it is not working as expected.  I watched the agent demo video, replicated the conversation shown in the demo, and our instance is not replying the way it should be,  based on the demo conversation. Rather, it gives me a very vague reply with no actual context on my direct report's work. 

 We have all the data sources enabled, and they have data points in them, so there is context that should be generated when I ask questions. 

Does anyone have this turned on, and have you experienced any issues? I'd love to hear your feedback and any roadblocks you have run into with this agent in Joule.

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

how am i able to change switch over the federation lead form one node to another?
Is there a possibility to release the federation lead role?

If i would add a federation peer that was a formerly federation lead (with no peers anymore), i get error 
"Peer [lxli03-022] is already Federation Lead for another SAP Host Agent Federation network [Lead CA: 'CN=LXLI03-022.res01.ads.ooe.local, OU=SAPHostAgent.INTCOM, OU=SAP Host Agent Certificate Authority, O=SAP Host Agent, C=DE']. Peer cannot be integrated into this SAP Host Agent Federation."

Also the "force" Parameter won't be an option, as the tooltip says "..if the peer is a federation lead itself, no trust can be set up."

Please give me a hint. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
Hello @Naveenk_S42024,

Thanks for your kind words. Regarding Account Assignment Category 'Asset' in CIM, below page does mention it. Possibly needs more setup. Will try to get more details.
Invoice Detail Page: Sections and Fields

entry.title: Re: Compensation Statements in People Profile
entry.content:
To add on to David's comment - the "older way" where you enable the portlet by adding it to the People Profile but the access to the statement itself is done through either Manage Statement Templates and adjusting the default settings or doing it at the template and statement level itself. Visibility of the statements is controlled via the comp module, not RBP.

 

 

Default permissions = whatever is set in the screen above.

Change permissions for who can view generated statements = same as before this can be used to override the RBP permissions.

Generated statements are not visible = same as before, nobody can see them.

Using the above method - you can only grant access via People Profile to users who fall into those standard categories - Employee, Manager, HR, Matrix, Custom and Second Manager.

If you wish to control access to the compensation statements using RBP, under Actions for All Plans > Company Settings you need to select "View generated Variable Pay Statements, Variable Pay Individual View, and Combined Statements based on Role-Based Permissions". 

When you save this, the  button shown above in the "old way" for "Set Default Template Permissions" goes away and now you manage the permissions to grant statement access through RBP which is where the permission under "Employee Data" and Personal Compensation Statement (or Combined Statement) come into play. This allows you more flexibility to grant users access to statements that fall outside of the standard roles above and you can grant access to the statements via People Profile for such as the Comp Admin, for instance.

Of course regardless of the method that is chosen, this needs to be managed carefully as you may want to grant access to Managers at a different time than employees seeing their own statements - some people feel that managing this in RBP is more difficult than the "old way" where all of the checkboxes are together in 1 screen. The advantage of using RBP is the ability to grant the access more broadly where you may have reviewers that do not fit into the standard relationships. In either method you need to be aware of which roles grant this access so that you do not inadvertently permission access to someone who should not see the statement for an individual or grant access to employees too early.

When using the RBP method - the behaviour of the screen below changes

Default permissions = Role-Based Permissions

Change permissions for who can view generated statements = same as before this can be used to override the RBP permissions.

Generated statements are not visible = same as before, nobody can see them.

 

I hope this helps.

Thanks,

Lesley

entry.title: Double checks in one page from F110
entry.content:
Dear Sapients,

We have a requirement for a specific country to print 2 checks in a single page. Currently if the payment is generated for 2 suppliers, it creates 1 single spool with 2 pages. 

I've gone through the older posts and somebody had a similar requirement but for SAP Scripts. For Scripts, we had designed a custom driver program to handle it but with our new system, we've migrated to Adobe forms and it uses driver program SAPFPAYM. The calling logic in SAPFPAYM isn't as straightforward as RFFOUS_C. It calls a lot of FMs dynamically to build the dataset and call the form. I've also gone through the events in OBPM1 but I haven't found anything useful.

If anybody knows of a simpler way to handle this instead of creating a custom driver program, please do let me know. I'd be super grateful!

entry.title: Re: new story cannot be saved: "APP_UNAVAILABLE_SELECT_NEW"
entry.content: I found that saving a blank story first, or just adding the live model with no data then saving before adding a table. Then when you add a table it lets you save.

entry.title: Re: Running STAR report service to analyze authorizations.
entry.content: I can't see any instruction in that pdf. I am executing the program SLIM_USER_CLF_HELP, and using the rule set PCE v.1.69. It runs in the foreground but not in the bacground. Can you attach specific instruction on how to run in the background.

entry.title: How to get tax to determine based off tax material classification in Settlement Management
entry.content:
From research I understand the 

Tax condition type RETX has reference condition type TTX1 and has access sequence RET1 assigned to it. In the standard content for condition type TTX1, access sequence TTX1 is assigned. The two access sequences (RET1 and TTX1) have one access in common, called Domestic taxes, which contain the following access fields:

Technical name of the conditionField description
ALANDDeparture Country/Region
TAXK1Tax Classification 1-Customer
TAXM1Tax Classification Material

 

I understand by default the tax classification material is set to 1 - How do I get the tax classification to read from the product master data in the settlement management type 0S01 - Sales Rebate ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Tutorial - Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent

entry.title: Prevent EWM Wave Release if No Staging Area Is Determined
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I need some guidance on the following topic.

I have deliberately not fully maintained STADET_OUT. As a result, when I simulate a wave, the system creates a warehouse task where the destination bin is the same as the source bin, because no staging area can be determined. This behavior is expected.

However, I want to prevent users from releasing the wave in this situation. Ideally, the wave should be blocked from release, or wave determination should not be possible at all, when no staging area can be determined.

Also, is there a BAdI available to determine the queue based on the wave template?

Any insights or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Join a Live Session this month and boost your skills
entry.content:
We have some great topics lined up for February 2026.  Click on the links to see the dates/times and register for our 1 hour webinars to learn with the experts.

From Table to Tile - how to create a custom KPI with SAP S/4HANA

Getting Started with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph

Building Cubes in Eclipse using ABAP Core Data Services

Developing Hierarchies for Analytical Consumption using ABAP CDS

entry.title: Re: Fiori Migration to SAP BTP: Best Practice for Multi-B...
entry.content:
Hi,

calling all backends and aggregating results on the UI can hardly be an efficient and proper solution (performance, security, error handling if one/multiple backend not available).

Alternative: route each user's request to their specific regional backend. The flow:

  1. User authenticates via SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service)
  2. IAS includes a custom user attribute (e.g., region = EMEA) in the JWT token
  3. A thin middleware layer (CAP application or approuter extension) reads the region claim
  4. The middleware selects the correct BTP destination (ERP_EMEA, ERP_ASIA, or ERP_AMER) and proxies the OData request to that single backend

BTP building blocks that support this:

  • BTP Destination Service supports runtime destination resolution without application restarts. Destinations can also be flagged with HTML5.DynamicDestination = true for dynamic selection
  • SAP IAS supports custom schema attributes on users, which propagate into JWT tokens - this is where the region claim would live
  • SAP CAP  supports dynamic data source routing. A blog by Shanthakumar Krishnaswamy describes switching backends on the fly based on region

This is a well-established pattern outside SAP. The approach is known as "claims-based routing" or "tenant-aware routing" in multi-tenant SaaS architecture:

  • Azure provides an official sample - a reverse proxy reads the tenant claim from the JWT and routes to the correct backend
  • AWS documents tenant routing strategies for SaaS, covers silo-model routing where each tenant/region has a dedicated backend
  • Istio supports natively at the service mesh level

Your regional ECC systems are effectively "silos" in SaaS terminology, and the user's region is the "tenant claim".

For multi-region considerations, SAP provides a reference architecture and sample code.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QUSS How to check change logs for custom business configuration?
entry.content:
Hi David, seems this question is a duplicate of https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/coe-quss-how-to-check-change-logs-for-custom-business-configuration/qaq-p/14324001

Do you mean this app? 

From: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/76384d8e68e646d6ae5ce8977412cbb4.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

to this app?:https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/business-configuration-change-logs?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

Display Change Logs

For a selected business configuration, the action Display Change Logs is available in the header section if:

  • at least one database table of the RAP BO of the Business Configuration Maintenance Object Table Logging is active.

  • the Business Configuration Change Logs app is configured as a valid navigation target for the user.

With this action, you can navigate to the Business Configuration Change Logs app.

Could you explain why it is not available in your system? do you have the proper roles, etc?

"

To access the app, you need to have the following business catalog assigned to your user: SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_LOG_PC - Business Configuration - Change Logs.

This business catalog is available in the business role template: SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT.

To display the change logs for tables, users must have the authorization for the object S_TABU_NAM with Display Change Documents for ACTVT and the name of the table for TABLE

" 

If you could provide some more details, someone should be able to help

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur - User Profile Management
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Recommendations
  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub
  • AI Assisted E-Document Error Handling : Continuous Adaptation

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Checklist to Re-Establish SAP Fieldglass Integrations in Sandbox Environments
entry.content:
Thank you for sharing the guidance on refreshing the sandbox from the production environment.
Could you also please provide guidance on MSP/vendor account and credential setup? This is currently a very time‑consuming and challenging process, requiring significant effort to set up MSPs and vendor accounts for all markets.
In addition, Fieldglass production data such as rates, bid information, SOWs, etc. is highly sensitive and should not be copied to the sandbox environment, which is accessible to many team members. Clear instructions on how to handle this data during sandbox refreshes would be extremely helpful

entry.title: Re: Automated email that class added
entry.content:
Hi,

There are two APMs that work together for class requests. The Class Request Demand Met notification (ApmComponentRequest) notifies administrators/contacts when demand reaches the minimum enrollment threshold.

The Requested Class Availability Email Notification (ApmUserRequestedSONotification) is the learner-facing side - it automatically matches new class sessions against pending requests and emails the requestors with class details and a direct registration link. For setup instructions and prerequisites, see section 1.4.8 "Creating an Automatic Notification for Learners who Requested a Class" in the Managing Learning Notification Templates guide.

If you enable it and learners aren't receiving emails, check KBA 2357628, as it covers the common issues (need-by dates, class end dates, etc.).

entry.title: Re: How to automate NBP T-1 Exchange Rate logic in S/4HAN...
entry.content: This is completely wrong advice and against any SAP standard for Poland! If you start to redetermine translation date to -1, most of Polish DRC reports would not work correctly. You would also need to implement that switch everywhere, because this is not only the requirement for billing, but for every posting in foreign currency, excluding valuation. Please check note "3593194 - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Solution Strategy for SAF-T reporting (JPK) in Poland (SAP S/4HANA)" and the attachment that describes what is the Polish standard to store exchange rate to achieve -1 date switch. This can be done with MRM.

entry.title: Re: Manage RBP Admin and Super Admin Access page appears...
entry.content: We are experiencing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP Convergent Mediation and Convergent Charging cont...
entry.content: Hi Eric, Thanks for the response. I have sent email to Abhijeet and waiting for his response. He is for Convergent Charging support, what about Convergent Mediation containerization possibility? As per below official document Convergent Mediation will support AWS EKS but not sure whether it will support with HANA Database or not!!! https://infozone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/UEPE4D/pages/233284285/Deployment+Architecture+4.2 and https://infozone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/UEPE5D/pages/639666201/Set+Up+Kubernetes+Cluster+-+AWS+5.2 . I have also few more questions for SAP CC and CM Active-Active possibility with HANA Database. Is possible can you check and help on these asks.

entry.title: Re: Archiving B2B Messages from SAP Integration Suite in CMIS Repository
entry.content:
Hi Adarsh,

Its great to see that you were able to implement the archiving feature. We are also trying to archive the messages from Iflows to Opentext CMIS repository . I was able to activate the archiving but getting error for the job that executes daily. Response in the /api/v1/ArchivingRuns shows as <d:RunFailedPhase>INITIALIZATION</d:RunFailedPhase> and mentioned by SAP that ContentStreamAllowed property  in CMIS  is causing initialization to fail. Did you create separate subtype/document type in CMIS ? Would like to know if you have also faced something like this when setting up the archiving.

entry.title: Re: API Purchase Order Item Note
entry.content:
Hi Julien,

I don't have access to the system with that API, but if you can't (I assume) manipulate RAP BO implementation to make SAVE_TEXT to insert the text, then you could try to use a $batch request that DELETEs the existing note entity first, then POSTs the replacement text:

POST /sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/$batch

You'd want to GET the existing note first to obtain its key.

KBA 3604894 ("How to POST several OData V4 service entities at the same time") documents $batch support for this API and uses PO item notes as its example payload.

entry.title: Issue with San Marino Electronic Invoicing via SDI (S/4HANA 2023)
entry.content:
Hello,

We have successfully implemented the SDI (Sistema di Interscambio) for Italy on our S/4HANA 2023 system.

While the process is working correctly for domestic Italian customers, we are encountering an issue with customers based in San Marino. Although we are using the correct configuration (IT4 - Italy, IPA code: 2R4GTO8), these transactions are not appearing in the eDocument Cockpit (EDOC_COCKPIT).

I have checked the Value Mapping in transaction /AIF/VMAP, but I couldn't find any specific restrictions or missing entries blocking this flow.

Do you have any insights or recommendations on why these specific documents are not being triggered in the cockpit?



Philippe
FIN (Finance)   #Edocument

entry.title: SAP for Me Demo
entry.content:
SAP for Me is your personalized, central point of entry into SAP support. It gives you transparency across all your SAP solutions and ensures a consistent support experience. SAP for Me makes your support work easier with a comprehensive view of your product portfolio in one place.

Learn more about the SAP for Me Portal and the frequently used Get Support feature in this support webcast.

Register now

This would be really hepfull as currently the solution works only for new terminated employees!

Thanks 

Sevda

entry.title: Re: Mark role as “OBSOLETE” in PFCG
entry.content:
Hi Dmytro,

as 7.11 is very old, my strong suspicion is it just does not have that feature (and Copy, Print, Where-used list, Delete, Transport either). The entire PFCG Role menu was substantially enhanced in later SAP_BASIS releases (740+). The other system you're comparing to is probably more modern.

entry.title: Re: SAP Process Automation: Free Tier Availability
entry.content: In case anyone facing issue finding the SAP Build Process Automation booster or trying to install it from subaccount and it runs forever. Try to run the SAP Build Code Booster. After running this booster, I can work on the Build Process Automation.

entry.title: Re: How to get insights in the data footprint of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant
entry.content:
Hi @Claudia_Semmler 

Thank you for this blog post, it covers a lot of points that currently do not have much visibility.

Quick query:  Does the total GB value given in the Cloud ALM metric/card/tile "Top Tables: Table Sizes" shows the total used disk space at the OS level?

If not, how can I find that figure, as well as the DB (used disk) Growth Statistics over a year, per month?

You also mentioned C414 (DB size license measurement?); where I can view the same and current vs historical usage stats?

Thanks in advance and best regards,

Shaji

entry.title: I am not able to open any of my iflows created in Integration suite and it gives me an error
entry.content:
hello experts,

I am not able to open any of my iflows and it gives me an error " The Content given is not a valid Integration Flow Bundle content" . I can see there is a note - 2691717 which mentions there might not be enough space in the tenant node and that one case has to be raised in component BC-CP-IS-OPS. Can someone guide me how to do this?

entry.title: Re: How can i restrict access to custom CDS View Data by...
entry.content:
Hi,
In S/4HANA Public Cloud, the way to link a custom authorization object to a Business Role is through a Restriction Type.

1. Create a Restriction Type referencing ZVHAOBJ1 via the Maintain Restriction Types app
2. Assign the Restriction Type to a Business Catalog
3. Assign the Business Catalog to a Business Role
4. Configure the restriction values (supplier list, read activity) in the Business Role's Restrictions tab

George Yu's blog covers this flow end to end.

entry.title: Creating Simulation Template with start and end pages
entry.content:
Hello,

Could someone help me troubleshoot why my start and end pages appear as blank when published. I have previously followed  step-by-step instructions to create a new simulation template with start and end pages, and this worked for a while. But all the start and end pages associated with template now appear blank. 

 

Is there a way to fix this? If I have to create a new simulation template, how can I link it to the existing simulations?

Thanks,

Sanchia

entry.title: Re: what is the difference between Embedded Data Ation an...
entry.content:
Hi,

An "Embedded Data Action" isn't a separate type — it's a step type within a data action that calls another data action. It's for reusability (run the same logic across multiple data actions without duplicating it).

SAP Help doc.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Connect SAP Business Application Studio and...
entry.content: See also the troubleshooting note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3679283

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA on azure: Problems with ADT locking and ...
entry.content:
Unfortunately the error still persists... 😢

Time outs and locking errors.

entry.title: Design for Everyone: Our Unifying Principle
entry.content:
A collage advertising SAP's commitment to Accessibility, with the central text "SAP Design: Design for Everyone" in a blue overlay. The background shows various webpage images with titles like "About Accessibility," "Inclusive Research Handbook," and "Designing for Visual Impairment," alongside diverse photos of people and accessibility icons.

At SAP, our work is guided by a simple but powerful mission: to help the world run better and improve people’s lives. This begins with one unifying principle: designing for everyone. 

Discover in our brand-new video, SAP Design: Design for Everyone, how SAP’s foundational approach to accessibility leads to more intuitive products for everyone and underscores our commitment to human rights.

More Than a Checklist

Accessibility isn’t an afterthought or a formality to be checked off at the end—it is the foundation of how we build more inclusive products. It’s a philosophy that is deeply tied to our respect for human rights and our goal to build technology that genuinely serves all people, no matter who they are or how they interact with the world.

An Experience That Works for Everyone

This belief challenges us to move beyond checklists and technical requirements. It’s about shifting our perspective to be more empathetic and cultivate an inclusive experience mindset. When we design with inclusion in mind, we create products that are simpler, clearer, and more intuitive. The result is an experience that works better for everyone.

Commitment in Action

This is more than just a statement; it reflects our core values, which are deeply tied to our commitment to human rights. We bring this commitment to life through the thoughtful and continuous evolution of our products, with tangible offerings such as our Horizon visual theme, our accessible 72 font, and intuitive screen reader capabilities.

Want to Learn More About How SAP Designs for Everyone?

  • Watch our new video, SAP Design: Design for Everyone
  • Read how we are Building Empathy into Design
  • Discover our approach to Accessibility in AI
  • Download the Accessibility Design Tools Handbook - Second Edition

entry.title: Re: Features & functions of app Cash Flow Analyzer (F2332) in S/4HANA Cash and Liquidity Managem
entry.content:
Thx

One of the customers ask me if this App can be modified with adding required fields to the simulation view in the cash flow Analyzer Fiori App.
This functionality is critical for the customer’s daily operations.
 When accessing the cash concentration simulation view, we’re unable to add any additional fields to the report.
This capability is essential for customers, as they rely on these fields, such as the company code, when planning their daily cash pools.

Thx

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite issue with Deep Entity create: depe...
entry.content:
Well I solved by Myself as this looks like a limitation of XML to JSON converter, the items were getting themselves a new group tag <Entregas> for example, which in turn made the mechanism that processes the payload in the OData connector to read only the last group, instead of everything being under a single <Entregas>.

After checking note 2478282 and the XML to JSON Converter in INtegration Suite docs, i realized that this was a limitation of the tool. This might be solvable using a message mapping but at least for me it was easier to create a Groovy Script to do the parsing correctly, and it worked like a charm.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: The SSAM app performs a full sync instead of a delta sync on login after 1–3weeks of inactivity
entry.content:
Issue Summary

When users of SAP Service and Asset Manager (SSAM 2405) do not use the mobile application for 1–3 weeks, the next login triggers a full data synchronization instead of a delta sync, resulting in significantly longer sync times.

Detailed Description

  • Users have reported that after a period of inactivity (approximately 2–3 weeks), logging into the SSAM mobile application triggers a full synchronization.

  • Normally, the application performs a delta sync to update only changed records, which is faster and more efficient.

  • Due to the full sync, the login process takes an extended time, impacting productivity and delaying field operations.

  • This behavior is observed consistently across multiple devices and users who have been idle for an extended period.

Expected Behavior

  • After a period of inactivity, logging into SSAM should perform a delta sync (syncing only changed data), not a full sync, unless there are configuration or data inconsistencies requiring a full sync.

  • The sync time should remain comparable to normal delta sync durations.

Actual Behavior

  • After 1–3 weeks of inactivity, the application performs a full sync, which significantly increases login and data retrieval time.

Impact

  • Reduced user productivity due to long sync times.

  • Delays in field operations and work order processing.

  • Increased network and backend load during full sync events.

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Do not log in to the SSAM mobile application for 2–3 weeks.

  2. Open the SSAM mobile app and log in.

  3. Observe that the application performs a full sync instead of a delta sync.

  4. Note the extended sync duration compared to normal delta sync operations.

Request / Questions for SAP Support

  • Is this behavior expected for extended idle periods, or is it a potential issue with delta sync?

  • Are there configuration settings or best practices to ensure that the application performs a delta sync even after long periods of inactivity?

  • Is there a recommended way to minimize sync time for users returning after prolonged inactivity?

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - error 4
entry.content:
This note helped me: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3679283

Using S4HC tracing, it turned out that it was adding a "/" to the end of my Destination Audience URL. 

Works: https://my234567.s4hana.cloud.sap

Works not: https://my234567.s4hana.cloud.sap/

entry.title: Re: ADT HTTP Service created with error
entry.content:
@saurabh__khare38 any news on this? I ran into the same issue today.

BR Chris

entry.title: Re: ADT HTTP Service created with error
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

We raised an incident to SAP. They activated/re-activated the repository object type HTTP in SE80. This will ensure that the discovery is correctly generated. That resolved the issue.

Case for your reference 1446133/2025 

Also, note that for services created using ADT no SICF node is created. This is the new behavior from S/4H 2022 onwards. You need to activate the generated service via UCON_HTTP_SERVICES on GUI. This works irrespective of whether the service details are visible in the ADT editor or not.

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI: March 12, 2026
entry.content:
Managing incoming documents efficiently is critical for business operations. SAP Cloud ERP, powered by SAP Document AI, offers pre-delivered use cases to automate and optimize document processing across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.

In this live session, we’ll explore how SAP Document AI enhances document inbound processing, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy. You’ll see live demos of end-to-end workflows and learn how to activate these capabilities in your system.

Key Takeaways:

  • Discover pre-built document processing use cases in SAP Cloud ERP.
  • See live demos of end-to-end workflows for Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • Learn how to activate and extend these capabilities for your business needs.

Who Should Attend? Project managers, consultants, business users, and administrators looking to improve document processing efficiency.

Register Now!

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content:
Hello Team, I hope you all are doing well!

I prepared for the certification exam - SAP Certified Associate - Integration Developer. It shows " This SAP Certification Exam will be replaced with a practical exam very soon." since many weeks now. 

Can you please inform when the practical exam will be available? I have a fear of losing my knowledge and hand-on practice preparation if it is not going to be available soon. Thank you!

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA: Installation number "INITIAL"
entry.content:
Hi there!

My SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA running on azure should have the installation number "DEMOSYSTEM" but instead the installation number is "INITIAL".

Screenshot from System info:

Screenshot from SLICENSE:

 

 

With this configuration it is not possible to install ABAP Open Source Namespaces.

What am I doing wrong?

@sebastian_wolf

entry.title: The Future of Intelligent Hiring with SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors (Americas)
entry.content:
Hiring is evolving faster than ever – and we’re quickly moving toward a future that’s more intelligent, data-driven, and people-centric. To stay competitive, future-ready organizations must place insights, AI, and skills at the center of their talent strategies to make smarter hiring decisions and drive better business outcomes.

Join this session to learn how intelligent hiring helps organizations elevate talent experiences, accelerate hiring processes, and ensure every hire is aligned with business and workforce goals. Discover how SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors works alongside the SAP SuccessFactors Learning and Talent portfolio to fuel speed, quality, and business success – empowering your organization to build a workforce ready for what’s next.

Key topics include:

•    Emerging trends in talent acquisition, insights on how AI is transforming hiring, and what this means for your organization

•    SAP’s strategic AI and product investments that enable organizations to make smarter hiring and business decisions

•    Live demo showcasing AI-driven innovations in SmartRecruiters to attract, hire, and retain the best talent

Register now to experience the future of intelligent hiring – and discover how your organization can attract top talent faster, engage the best candidates, and hire the skills needed to stay ahead.

Register now

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP Platform 2023 developer edition on SAP HANA: Installation number "INITIAL"
entry.content:
I had to install a new license although the current license was valid.

https://go.support.sap.com/minisap/#/minisap

Now the system is set to DEMOSYSTEM

entry.title: Re: Error while sending email using custom email template using ABAP program.
entry.content: Have you solved the issue? I assume so, as it seems easy if you know the values of IT_DATA_KEY and IR_DATA of the terminated method CL_SMTG_EMAIL_RENDERER -> APPLY (those are visible in the dump).

entry.title: Re: Removing the select all icon from ALV
entry.content: These are two workarounds which both have big drawbacks! (Either remove the column headers or remove the row selection marks). There's no solution to disable this button.

entry.title: CL_GUI_ALV_GRID "select all" at grid top-left doesn't select all lines
entry.content:
The question was already asked here but was not answered.

I have a very simple editable ALV grid via CL_GUI_ALV_GRID, I have 3000 rows.

If I select all lines via the button "Select All" in the top left part of the grid (it's not in the toolbar, it's at the left of the column headers, above the column to select the rows), and I then click the button Delete in the toolbar to delete the lines, only around 300 rows are deleted.

So I have to repeat that 10 times to delete all rows.

What's the problem, how to select all rows?

My system: ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: CL_GUI_ALV_GRID "select all" at grid top-left doesn't select all lines
entry.content:
There's no standard solution.

One workaround is to add a custom button &ALL in the toolbar, it will select all rows as expected.

There's no way to hide the "Select All" button at the top left part of the grid (question already asked in the forum, for instance here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/removing-the-select-all-icon-from-alv/qaa-p/858449).
